questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Match the type of interaction in column A with the distance dependence of their interaction energy in column B:

A B
(i) ion - ion  (a) 1r
(ii) Dipole - dipole (b) 1r2
(iii) London dispersion (c) 1r3
  (iv) 1r6
questions.solution:
(i) ion-ion interaction energy is inversely proportional to the distance between ions 1r
(ii) dipole-dipole interaction energy is inversely proportional to the third power of r1r3
(iii) The interaction energy of London force is inversely proportional to sixth power of distance between two interaction particles 1r6
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: If the magnetic moment of a di-oxygen species is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1.73 </mn><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>.</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mo>.</mo></math>, it may be
questions.solution:
Given, magnetic moment of di-oxygen species=1.73 B.M.

We know, magnetic moment = n n+2 , where n=number of unpaired electrons

1.73= n n+2

n=1
So, species should contain one unpaired electron.

Let us check by electronic configuration of each species

O2=σ1s2 σ*1s2 σ2s2 σ*2s2 σ2p2z π2p2x=π2p2y π*2p1x=π*2p1yO2+=σ1s2 σ*1s2 σ2s2 σ*2s2 σ2p2z π2p2x=π2p2y π*2p1x=π*2p0yO2-=σ1s2 σ*1s2 σ2s2 σ*2s2 σ2p2z π2p2x=π2p2y π*2p2x=π*2p1y
 

As clear by above electronic configurations that, O2 has two unpaired electrons and O2- & O2+  Each has only one unpaired electron; hence magnetic moment of  O2+ & O2- is 1.73 B.M.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Which of the following are isostructural pairs?

A. SO42- and CrO42-

B. SiCl4 and TiCl4

C. NH3 and NO3-

D. BCl3 and BrCl3
questions.solution:
Isostructural species are those which have the same shape and hybridization.

Number of election pair =12[N+V−C+A]
Where
V= Number of valence electron present in central atom
N= Number of non-monovalent atoms bonded to covalent atom
C= Charge of cation
A= Charge of anion

Hybridisation can be calculated by using this formula then we will be able to find the structure from hybridisation.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Number of electron deficient molecules among the following <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>PH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>6</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>CCl</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi>LiH</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>BCl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> is
questions.solution:
A molecule is said to be electron deficient because there are not enough valence electrons to form the expected number of covalent bonds. For example, in B2H6, there are only 12 electrons, three from each boron atoms and six from hydrogen, while ethane possesses 14 such electrons.

Also in BCl3, B has six electrons in its outermost orbit. So, the number of electron deficient molecules are 2.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Match List-I with List-II

  List-I   List-II
A ΨMO=ΨA-ΨB I Dipole moment
B μ=Q×r II Bonding molecular orbital
C Nb-Na2 III Anti-bonding molecualr orbital
D ΨMO=ΨA+ΨB IV Bond order
questions.solution:
The molecular orbital σ formed by the addition of atomic orbitals is called the bonding molecular orbital while the molecular orbital σ* formed by the subtraction of atomic orbital is called antibonding molecular orbital.

The electron density in a bonding molecular orbital is located between the nuclei of the bonded atoms because of which the repulsion between the nuclei is very less while in case of an antibonding molecular orbital, most of the electron density is located away from the space between the nuclei. Infact, there is a nodal plane (on which the electron density is zero) between the nuclei and hence the repulsion between the nuclei is high.

  List-I   List-II
A ΨMO=ΨA-ΨB I ABMO
B μ=Q×r II Dipole moment
C Nb-Na2 III Bond order
D ΨMO=ΨA+ΨB IV BMO
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: Consider the species CH4, NH4+ and BH4-. Choose the correct option with respect to the there species:
options: They are isoelectronic and only two have tetrahedral structures
They are isoelectronic and all have tetrahedral structures
Only two are isoelectronic and all have tetrahedral structures
Only two are isoelectronic and only two have tetrahedral structures
questions.solution:
In CH4, NH4+, BH4- the number of electrons are 10 for each.

Hence, all the three species given are isoelectronic, and the central atom shows sp3 hybridization. Hence, they have tetrahedral structure.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The bond order and magnetic property of acetylide ion are same as that of
questions.solution:
The bond order of the acetylide ion HC≡C- is 3, indicating a triple bond between the carbon atoms.

The acetylide ion consists of two carbon atoms with a triple bond between them and a negative charge. In the triple bond, there are six bonding electrons (two pi bonds and one sigma bond) and zero antibonding electrons.

Among the given options NO+ will have the same bond order and magnetic property like acetylide ion.

Species Bond Order Magnetic moment
HC≡C- 3 0
O2+ 2.5 3 B.M
N2+ 2.5 3 B.M
NO+ 3 0
O2- 1.5 3 B.M

questions.q:
The ionic radii of F- and O2- respectively are 1.33 A and 1.4A, while the covalent radius of N is 0.74A 

The correct statement for the ionic radius of N3- from the following is :
options: It is smaller than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mo>-</mo></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math>

It is bigger than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mo>-</mo></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math>, but smaller than of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup></math>

It is smaller than O2- and F-, but bigger than of
N
questions.solution:
F-,O2- and N3- all are isoelectronic species in which N3- have least number of protons due to which it's size increases as least nuclear attraction
is experienced by the outer shell electrons. Size order N3->O2->F-

a 1s22s2 p 801
b 1s22s22p4 q 899
c 1s22s22p3 r 1314
d 1s22s22p1 s 1402

(a) 1s22s2→Be

(b) 1s22s22p4→O

(c) 1s22s22p3→N

The ionization enthalpy order is B<Be<O<N

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: What is the correct oder of electron gain enthalpy of Cl, F, Te, Po
questions.solution:
Electron gain enthalpy values of the elements are given below in kJ/mol,

FCl Te  Po −333−349−190−174.

Down the group electron affinity decreases. The size of fluorine atom is small. The addition of electron to small fluorine atom results in electron-electron repulsions. Hence, fluorine has less electron affinity than chlorine.

Hence, the correct order is 

Cl>F>Te>PO
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Element "<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi></math>" belongs to the period <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math> and group <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>16</mn></math> of the periodic table. The valence shell electron configuration of the element, which is just above "<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi></math>" in the group is
questions.solution:
General outer electronic configuration of 16th group elements is ns2np4

16th group element belonging to 4th period is Se and its outer electronic configuration is 4s24p4. The element present above selenium is sulphur and the outer electronic configuration of sulphur is 3s23p4.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The first ionization enthalpy of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Na</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>Mg</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Si</mi></math>, respectively, are: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>496</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>737</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>786</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kJ</mi><msup><mrow><mi>mo</mi><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The first ionization enthalpy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><msup><mi>kJmol</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mfenced></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Al</mi></math> is
questions.solution: In the third period, Al comes after Mg and before Si. Since in Mg (1s2 2s2 2p6 3s2 ), the last electron is to be lost from a fully filled 3s-orbital while in Al (ls2 2s2 2p6 3s2 3p1 ) it is to be lost from a 3p-orbital, therefore, ∆iH1 of Al is lower than that of Mg (737 kJ mol-1). Further, nuclear charge increases in going from Na to Al, therefore ∆iH1 of Al is higher than that of Na(496 kJ mol-1) but lower than that of Mg (737 kJ mol-1). Therefore, the appropriate value of ionisation potential of  Al is 577 kJ mol-1.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

Electron gain enthalpy becomes more negative across a period and becomes less negative as we go down the group. As all the pairs of elements given from comparision, each pair of element belong to the same group. Based on the fact option C is incorrect. Data for electron gain enthalpy is given below:
(A) ΔegH(Cl)<ΔegH(F)

   (-345)        (-328) correct

(B) ΔegH(Se)<ΔegH(S)

   (-195)     (-200) Incorrect

(C) ΔegH(I)<ΔegH(At) 

   (-295)      (-270) correct

(D) ΔegH(Te)<ΔegH(Po) 

    (-190)      (-183)correct
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: For compound having the formula <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>GaAlCl</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math>, the correct option from the following is
options: Ga is coordinated with CI in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>GaAlCl</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math>
Ga is more electronegative than AI and is present as a cationic part of the salt <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>GaAlCl</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math>
CI forms bond with both AI and Ga in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>GaAlCl</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math>
Oxidation state of Ga in the salt <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>GaAlCl</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo> </mo><mtext>is</mtext><mo> </mo><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></math>
questions.solution:
Gallous tetrachloro aluminate (GaAlCl4) exists as Ga+AlCl4-.

Due to the presence of d-orbital in the case of Ga, the shielding effect of the d-orbital electrons is very poor so the size of the Ga atom is smaller than expected and so it can attract the shared pairs of electrons more effectively. Hence, the electronegativity of Ga is more than Al.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: . Identify the correct order of standard enthalpy of formation of sodium halides.
questions.solution:
The lattice energy of an ionic compound is the energy required to separate one mole of the compound into its constituent ions in the gas phase.

 Lattice energy∝1r++r-

The lattice energy is inversely proportional to the sum of the ionic radii r++r- of the ions involved. When the size of the ions increases, the distance between them also increases, leading to a decrease in the attractive forces between the ions and a decrease in the lattice energy. Therefore, if the size of the ions increases, the lattice energy generally decreases.

So Order : NaF > NaCl > NaBr > NaI
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q:
Simplified absorption spectra of three complexes ((i) and (ii) and (iii)) of M+n ion are provided below; their λmax values are marked as A, B and C respectively. The correct match between the complexes and their λmax values is:

(i) M(NCS)6(-6+n)
(ii) MF6(-6+n)
(iii) MNH36n+
questions.solution:
Stronger the ligand greater is splitting of d orbitals and smaller will be wave length of light absorbed.
The splitting power of ligands isNH3>NCS->F- So order of wave length of light absorbed is λNH3<λNCS¯<λF-

questions.q: The number of isomers possible for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mi>Pt</mi><mfenced><mi>en</mi></mfenced><msub><mfenced><msub><mi>NO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> is:
questions.solution:
PtenNO22

So, total possible isomers are 3.

The existence of coordination compounds with the same formula but different arrangements of the ligands was crucial in the development of coordination chemistry. Two or more compounds with the same formula but different arrangements of the atoms are called isomers.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The Crystal Field Stabilization Energy (CFSE) and magnetic moment (spin-only) of an octahedral aqua complex of a metal ion <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mrow><mi>Z</mi><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></mfenced></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>-</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>8</mn><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">Δ</mi><mn>0</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>87</mn></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>BM</mi></math>, respectively. Identify <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mrow><mi>Z</mi><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></mfenced><mo>:</mo></math>
questions.solution:

questions.q:
Arrange the following metal complex/ compounds in the increasing order of spin only magnetic moment. Presume all the three, high spin system.

(Atomic numbers Ce=58, Gd=64 and Eu=63.)

(a) NH42CeNO36

(b) GdNO33 and

(c) EuNO33
questions.solution:
(a) Ce58→Xe4f25 d06 s2

In complex Ce4+→Xe4f05 d06 s0

there is no unpaired electron so μm=0

(b) Gd643+→Xe4f75 d06 s0

contain seven unpaired electrons so, μm=77+2=63 B.M.

(c) Eu633+→Xe544f65d06s0

contain six unpaired electron 

so, μm=66+2=48 B.M.

Hence, order of spin only magnetic movement 

b>c>a

questions.q: The calculated magnetic moments (spin only value) for species <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><msub><mi>FeCl</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mi>Co</mi><msub><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mn>3</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mi>MnO</mi><mn>4</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> respectively are:
questions.solution:
(i) FeCl42- Fe2+ 3d6→4 unpaired electrons.

μ=nn+2BM

=44+2BM

=24 BM⇒4.90 BM

 

(ii) CoC2O43-3 is a strong field ligand & causes
pairing & hence no. unpaired electron

μ=0

 

(iii) MnO42-  Mn+6 it has one unpaired electron.

Mn+6⇒Ar3 d1  μ=nn+2BM

=11+2BM

=3BM⇒1.73 BM

questions.solution:
The white precipitate silver chloride is soluble in ammonia and forms the complex with coordination number 2. The complex formation is shown below.

AgCl(s)+2NH3(aq)→AgNH32Cl(aq)

It is ammoniacal silver chloride.

CuCN4-3

questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> cation gives a prussian blue precipitate on addition of potassium ferrocyanide solution due to the formation of
questions.solution:
Ferric ion forms a prussian blue precipitate due to the formation of Fe4FeCN63 . In this method, to a portion of sodium fusion extract, freshly prepared ferrous sulphate FeSO4 solution is added and warmed. Then about 2 to 3 drops of FeCl3 solution are added and acidified with conc. HCl . The appearance of a prussian blue colour indicates the presence of nitrogen.

FeSO4+2NaOH→FeOH2+Na2SO4

6NaCN+Fe ( OH ) 2 → N a 4 [ Fe ( CN ) 6 ] Sodium ferrocyanide +2NaOH

3N a 4 [ Fe ( CN ) 6 ]+4FeC l 3 → F e 4 [ Fe ( CN ) 6 ] 3 Ferric ferrocyanide ( prussian blue ) +12NaCl

questions.solution:

CoCN63-; t2g6eg0; CFSE =-0.4×6+0.6×0Δo+2P=−2.4Δo+2P

Since CN-1 is a strong field ligand, Δo of this complex will be higher than that of CoH2O63+

questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Match List-I with List-II

  List-I
(Complex)		   List-II
(Hybridization)
A NiCO4 I sp3
B NiCN42- II sp3d2
C CoCN63- III d2sp3
D CoF63- IV dsp2

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
questions.solution:
Ni is in zero oxidation state in Ni(CO)4 so the electronic configuration of Ni is 3d84s2. As CO is a strong ligand, it pushes all the electrons in the 3d orbital, therefore the hybridisation of Ni(CO)4 is sp3 and it has tetrahedral geometry. It is diamagnetic due to the absence of unpaired electrons.

In [Ni(CN)4]2-, there is Ni2+ ion for which the electronic configuration in the valence shell is 3d84s0.

In presence of strong field CN- ions, all the electrons are paired up.

The empty, 3d, 3s and two 4p Orbitals undergo dsp2 hybridization to make bonds with CN- ligands in square planar geometry.

The atomic number of Co is 27 and its valence shell electronic configuration is 3d74s2.

Co is in +3 oxidation state in the complex [CoF6]3-.

[CoF6]3- is sp3d2 hybridized and it is octahedral in shape.

Another Co3+ complex, [Co(CN)6]3-, is diamagnetic and has no unpaired electrons. The hybrid orbitals used to form this complex are d2sp3.

NiCO4 Hybridisation sp3

NiCN42- Hybridisation dsp2

CoCN63- Hybridisation d2Sp3

CoF63- Hybridisation sp3d2
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q:
1 L,0.02M solution of CoNH35SO4Br is mixed with 1 L,0.02M solution of CoNH35BrSO4. The resulting solution is divided into two equal parts X and treated with excess AgNO3 solution and BaCl2 solution respectively as shown below:
1 L Solution X+AgNO3 solution (excess) →Y
1 L Solution X+BaCl2 solution (excess) →Z
The number of moles of Y and Z respectively are
questions.solution:
The number of moles of  CoNH35SO4Br=0.02×1=0.02 mol 

The number of moles of   CoNH35BrSO4=0.02×1=0.02 mol

The total volume of the solution becomes two Litres after mixing.

Following precipitation reactions occur:
CoNH35SO4Br+AgNO3→AgBr↓0.01mol excess 0.01 mol

CoNH35BrSO4+BaCl2→BaSO4↓0.01mol excess 0.01 mol

questions.q:
Match List-I with List-II.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
d-orbital is degenerate which split into two levels eg and t2g in the presence of ligands. This splitting is due to the presence of ligands. This is called the crystal-field splitting and the energy difference between the two levels is called the crystal-field splitting energy, Δ0.

(A)  Ti2+:4s03d2

t2g e–=2  eg e–=0 

Δ0=2-.4Δ0=-.8Δ0

(B)  V2+:4s03d3

t2g e–=3  eg e–=0 

Δ0=3-.4Δ0

= -1.2Δ0

(C)  Mn3+=4s03d4

t2g e–=3eg e–=1 

Δ0=3-.4Δ0+1.6Δ0

=-1.2Δ0+0.6Δ0

= -0.6Δ0

(D)  Fe3+=4 s03d5

t2g e–=3  eg e–=2 

Δ0=3-.4Δ0+2.6Δ0

=-1.2Δ0+1.2Δ0=0
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: The complex that dissolves in water is
questions.solution:
Fe3(OH)2(OAc)6Cl is a coordination complex containing iron ions coordinated with hydroxide and acetate ligands. When dissolved in water, this complex can undergo hydrolysis and dissociation. 

So, Fe3(OH)2(OAc)6Cl dissolves in water. Rest of the complexes form precipitate.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
If Ni2+ is replaced by Pt2+ in the complex NiCl2Br22-, which of the following properties are expected to get changed?
A. Geometry
B. Geometrical isomerism
C. Optical isomerism
D. Magnetic properties
questions.solution:
When Ni2+ is replaced by Pt2+ in the complex NiCl2Br22-, the following properties are expected to be changed:

A. Geometry: Since Pt2+ and Ni2+ have different coordination preferences and atomic sizes, the replacement is likely to result in a change in the geometry of the complex. That is it changes to square planar.

B. Geometrical isomerism: If the replacement of Ni2+ with Pt2+ leads to a change in the ligand arrangement, it can result in the generation of cis and trans isomers.

C. Magnetic properties: PtCl2Br22- is diamagnetic.

Both the complex species are optically inactive.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Match List I with List II

  List I Complex   List II CFSE ∆0
A. CuNH362+ I. -0.6
B. TiH2O63+ II. -2.0
C. FeCN63- III. -1.2
D. NiF64- IV. -0.4

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
CFSE=(-0.4 nt2g+0.6neg)∆0

nt2g= Number of electrons in t2g orbital

neg=number of electrons in eg orbital.

Crystal Field Stabilisation Energy for the given complexes is as follows:

(A)

CuNH362+

Cu2+.. 3d6, t2g6 eg3

CFSE

=-6×0.4+3×0.6∆0

=-0.6∆0
(B)

TiH2O63+

Ti3+: 3d1, t2g1 eg0

CFSE=-1×0.4∆0

=-0.4∆0
(C)

FeCN63-

Fe3+:3d5, t2g5 eg0

CFSE=-5×0.4∆0

=-2.0 ∆0
(D)

NiF64-

Ni2+:3d8, t2g6 eg2

CFSE

=-6×0.4+2×0.6∆0

=-1.2 ∆0

So the correct option is B.

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: The metal ion (in gaseous state) with lowest spin-only magnetic moment value is
questions.solution:
Ion Electronic configuration No. of unpaired electron
1 Cr2+ 3d4 4
2 V2+ 3d3 3
3 Fe2+ 3d6 4
4 Ni2+ 3d8 2

Magnetic moment(spin only) is calculated by the formula μ= n(n+2) BM.

Magnetic moment (spin only)is directly proportional to the number of unpaired electrons.

Hence, less is the number of unpaired electrons less is the magnetic moment (spin only).

As Ni2+ has the least number of unpaired electrons it has the least magnetic moment (spin only) among the given options.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Highest oxidation state of Mn is exhibited in Mn2O7. The correct statements about Mn2O7 are

(A) Mn is tetrahedrally surrounded by oxygen atoms
(B) Mn is octahedrally surrounded by oxygen atoms
(C) Contains Mn-O-Mn bridge
(D) Contains Mn-Mn bond.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
questions.solution:
Each Mn is tetrahedrally surrounded by oxygen atoms. In addition, there is a bridging two-coordinated oxygen atom between two tetrahedral units. The bridged Mn-O has different bond length than the tetrahedrally surrounded Mn-O bond lengths.

 

So, the correct statement is only A and C.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A solution of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CrO</mi><mn>5</mn></msub></math> in amyl alcohol has a....colour
questions.solution: Chromium peroxide <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><msub><mi>CrO</mi><mn>5</mn></msub></mfenced></math> is a very unstable deep blue compound. It is soluble in water, but rapidly breaks down, and soluble as well as stable in solvents such as diethyl ether, ethyl acetate, amyl alcohol and its esters.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
The set of correct statements is:

(i) Manganese exhibits +7 oxidation state in its oxide.

(ii) Ruthenium and Osmium exhibit +8 oxidation in their oxides.

(iii) Sc shows +4 oxidation state which is oxidizing in nature.

(iv) Cr shows oxidising nature in +6 oxidation state.
questions.solution:
(i) The oxidation state of Mn in oxide (Mn2O7) is:

Let us consider Mn as x.

⇒ 2x + 7(-2) = 0

⇒ 2x + (-14) = 0

⇒ 2x -14 = 0

⇒ 2x = 14

⇒x = 7

(ii) The oxidation state of Ruthenium in oxide (RuO4) is:

Consider Ru as x.

⇒ x + 4(-2) = 0

⇒ x - 8 =0

⇒ x = 8

The oxidation state of Osmium in oxide (OsO4) is:

Consider Os as x.

⇒ x + 4(-2) = 0

⇒ x - 8 =0

⇒ x =  8  

Hence, Ruthenium and Osmium has +7 oxidation state in its oxide.

(iii) Sc has ns2 (n-1)d1 electronic configuration.

It utilizes two electrons from its ns sub-shell then its oxidation state = +2. When it utilizes both the electrons then its oxidation state = +3.

(iv) In the modern periodic table, chromium metal is present in group 6. It is a transition metal and belongs to the first transition series. The atomic number of chromium is 24. Its electronic configuration is [Ar] 3d5 4s1. 

It has five electrons in the 3d sub-shell and one electron in the 4s sub-shell. Thus, chromium metal has six valence electrons. Chromium can lose some or all of its valence electrons to form ions with different oxidation states. Thus, chromium shows the oxidation states of +1, +2, +3, +4, +5, and +6 respectively.
The most common oxidation states of chromium are +2, +3, and +6.

So, the statement (i), (ii) and (iv) are correct.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I : Aqueous solution of K2Cr2O7 is preferred as a primary standard in volumetric analysis over Na2Cr2O7 aqueous solution.

Statement II : K2Cr2O7 has a higher solubility in water than Na2Cr2O7. In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options: Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> is false but Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> is true
Both Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> and Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> are true
Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> is true but Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> is false
Both Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> and Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> are false
questions.solution: K2Cr2O7 is generally preferred than Na2Cr2O7 in volumetric analysis as Na2Cr2O7 is hygroscopic in nature, therefore it is difficult to prepare its standard solution for volumetric analysis as accurate weighing is not possible in normal atmospheric conditions. A hygroscopic substance can absorb moisture from the atmosphere due to which there will be inaccuracy in weight.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Prolonged heating is avoided during the preparation of ferrous ammonium sulphate to
questions.solution:
The formula for ferrous ammonium sulphate is FeSO4.(NH4)2SO4.6H2O. It is prepared by dissolving equimolar mixture of hydrated ferrous sulphate and ammonium sulphate in water containing a little sulphuric acid. The solution is subjected to crystallisation, ferrous ammonium sulphate separates out from the solution.

The chemical reaction is given below.

FeSO4 + (NH4)2SO4 + 6H2O → FeSO4.(NH4)2SO4.6H2O (Mohr’s salt)

Avoid prolonged heating while preparing crystals of ferrous ammonium sulphate, as it may oxidise ferrous ions to ferric ions and change the stoichiometry of the crystals.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: When a solution of mixture having two inorganic salts was treated with freshly prepared ferrous sulphate in acidic medium, a dark brown ring was formed whereas on treatment with neutral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>FeCl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math>, it gave deep red colour which disappeared on boiling and a brown red ppt was formed. The mixture contains
questions.solution: The brown ring test is a typical nitrate test that involves adding iron(II) sulphate to a nitrate solution, then slowly adding strong sulfuric acid until the acid forms a layer below the aqueous solution. The existence of the nitrate ion will be shown by the formation of a brown ring at the junction of the two layers. Dark brown ring is formed in the confirmatory test of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mi>NO</mi><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup></math> − ions. Deep red precipitate with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>FeCl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> is formed in the presence of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><msup><mi>COO</mi><mo>-</mo></msup></math> ions.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The Lewis acid character of boron tri halides follows the order:
questions.solution:
The strength of the acidic character of boron trihalides depends upon π-π back bonding. In boron trihalides, π-π -back bonding occurs due to empty orbital of boron and filled orbitals of halogens.

 

The π-π back bonding is shown maximum byBF3, as the size of B and F are small and comparatively same. Due to this effect tendency of accepting lone-pair of electrons of boron decreases as the size of halogen increases. The order of size of halogens are F < Cl < Br < I . Thus, acidic nature is in order BF3<BCl3<BBr3<Bl3.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Structures of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>BeCl</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> in solid state, vapour phase and at very high temperature respectively are:
questions.solution:
BeCl2 is dimeric in vapour phase. The coordination number of beryllium is 3.

BeCl2 is monomeric at high temperature. The coordination number of beryllium is 2.

BeCl2 is polymeric in solid state. The coordination number of beryllium is 4.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: The correct statement with respect to dinitrogen is:
options: it can combine with dioxygen at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>
liquid dinitrogen is not used in cryosurgery.
it can be used as an inert diluent for reactive chemicals
questions.solution:
(1) liquid dinitrogen is used in cryosurgery.

(2) N2 is diamagnetic in nature.

(3) it can not combine with dioxygen at 25ºC.

(4) is correct

questions.q: The radius of the second Bohr orbit, in terms of the Bohr radius, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mo>,</mo></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Li</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> is:
questions.solution:
Radius of Bohr's orbit is given as,

r=a0n2Z

n = orbit number

Z = atomic number

For 2nd Bohr's orbit of Li 2+

Li2+,r=a0223=4a03
